fields.description:
## Why
Make the long-running service observable and make every failure LOUD and troubleshootable тАФ hangs, errors, and stuck states must be highly visible, per the operability requirement. Also serve the remote liveness/queue surface `land` reads. Split out of S5 (`perpetual-ultrasonic-raccoon`).

## Files
- `infra/autolander/loop.py` тАФ EDIT: the read-only HTTP status endpoint + the `AUTOLANDER_ERROR` marker emission + the time-to-land metric emit.
- `infra/scripts/observability.sh` тАФ EDIT: the `autolander_errors` + `autolander_time_to_land_seconds` `PutMetricData` feeders.
- `infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf` тАФ NEW: the alarms.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **Read-only status endpoint (prior art: Zuul `zuul-web`, Tide `deck`):** a small read-only HTTP endpoint on the bot serving JSON тАФ the current `wipChain` (change, `phase`, `tested_shas`, **time-in-phase**), the count of `Autosubmit`-set-and-waiting changes, and **`heartbeat_age_s`** (integer seconds since the last heartbeat write тАФ the exact field `land`'s `lander_down` binds to). Read-only, non-secret; bound so agents can reach it.
- [ ] **Error metric (LOUD by default):** `observability.sh` publishes `rebar/host:autolander_errors` (`PutMetricData`) from THREE feeders, each a concrete bot-emitted signal: (1) the `AUTOLANDER_ERROR` structured stdout marker (the loop writes it on **any** unhandled error, then hands the current stack back rather than wedging silently); (2) an `AUTOLANDER_HANDBACK` structured stdout marker (the loop writes it on every stack hand-back тАФ since a hand-back, not the client-side `lander_down`, is what the bot itself can emit); (3) the container `unhealthy` Docker health-state (via `docker inspect --format '{{.State.Health.Status}}'`, NOT a journald grep). Alarm in `monitoring_autolander.tf`.
- [ ] **Stuck-state alarm (high visibility) тАФ as a CloudWatch metric, not an HTTP field:** the loop publishes a `autolander_time_in_phase_seconds` metric (`PutMetricData`, the current `wipChain`'s seconds-in-phase, emitted each poll while non-`idle`); `monitoring_autolander.tf` defines a CloudWatch alarm on THAT metric (alarms watch metrics, not the status-endpoint JSON). This makes a wedged-but-alive bot (a hang autoheal doesn't catch) visible.
- [ ] **Time-to-land metric:** emit `autolander_time_to_land_seconds` (explicit metric name) as **individual `Value` datapoints** per landed change (NOT `StatisticValues` тАФ CloudWatch computes extended-statistic percentiles only from raw `Value` datapoints); p50/p90/p99 come from CloudWatch extended statistics downstream (no hard-coded buckets). **In-session proof** (concrete): a unit test asserts the `PutMetricData` call is built with a single `Value` (not `StatisticValues`) and the `autolander_time_to_land_seconds` name; populated percentiles are an expected post-deploy outcome, not an in-sprint deliverable.
- [ ] The service logs to container stdout and NEVER writes `session_log`.
- [ ] **Tests:** the status endpoint returns the documented JSON shape (incl. `heartbeat_age_s`); a forced error produces an `AUTOLANDER_ERROR` marker + a metric datapoint, and a forced hand-back produces an `AUTOLANDER_HANDBACK` marker; the `autolander_time_in_phase_seconds` publisher and the `monitoring_autolander.tf` alarm-on-that-metric expression are unit-checked; the time-to-land `PutMetricData` call is asserted to use a single `Value` with the right metric name.
